expectations.text: Structures the assessment around the GOVERN categories (policies/processes; accountability structures; workforce diversity/culture; organizational engagement; third-party risk; monitoring/inventory-type categories) with a rating per area — consistent with the six GOVERN categories of AI RMF 1.0
expectations.evidence: Table and detailed sections rate GOVERN 1 through GOVERN 6, individually labeled: GOVERN 1 (policies/processes/risk tolerance), GOVERN 2 (accountability), GOVERN 3 (workforce diversity/competency), GOVERN 4 (organizational culture), GOVERN 5 (stakeholder engagement), GOVERN 6 (third-party/supply chain) — matches the real six-category structure well, each with a distinct maturity rating.

expectations.text: Correctly rates the described state: ethics statement = partial credit on culture/policy, while missing risk policy, missing cross-functional team, undefined risk tolerance, and absent legal involvement are each flagged as specific gaps against specific GOVERN areas
expectations.evidence: GOVERN 4 rated 'Initial' with rationale 'The ethics statement is genuine evidence of cultural intent — this is worth crediting'; GOVERN 1 flags 'No formal AI risk policy means there is no documented linkage'; GOVERN 2 flags 'No cross-functional AI team means there is no defined owner'; GOVERN 5 flags 'legal's total absence from AI deployment decisions is a direct, explicit failure of this category' — each prompt fact is mapped to specific categories.

expectations.text: Flags undefined risk tolerance as a foundational gap (GOVERN 1.x expects documented risk tolerance informing all downstream MAP/MEASURE/MANAGE work)
expectations.evidence: 'No defined risk tolerance is a critical gap — without it, no one in the organization can consistently decide what level of AI risk is acceptable' and 'GOVERN 1 is the anchor category — nearly every other GOVERN, MAP, MEASURE, and MANAGE activity depends on having tolerance thresholds and policy to measure against.'

expectations.text: Remediation plan is prioritised and sequenced (policy + accountability + tolerance first; team formation and legal integration next; inventory and third-party processes after) with owners/timeframes
expectations.evidence: Four-phase plan (Days 1-30, 31-60, 61-90, Q2/91-180) with P1/P2/P3 priority tags and a named Owner column per action item (e.g., 'CEO/COO sponsor', 'General Counsel', 'Working Group + Legal') — highly explicit sequencing and named accountable owners/timeframes throughout, arguably more granular than required.

expectations.text: Deliverables are concrete: AI risk policy outline, RACI or committee charter, risk tolerance statement examples, AI system inventory — not just abstract advice
expectations.evidence: The plan repeatedly references drafting a risk tolerance statement, RACI, and AI risk policy as action items (e.g., 'Draft a risk tolerance statement (even a first-draft, board-reviewable version)', 'Draft and approve a formal AI Risk Management Policy', 'Define and publish RACI for AI governance') but never actually delivers the content of these artifacts — no risk-policy outline with section headings, no RACI chart/charter skeleton, no example risk tolerance statement wording, and no AI inventory field list are provided anywhere in the response. This is abstract advice to go create these deliverables, not the concrete deliverables themselves, which the assertion specifically requires.
